page: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with hyper-threading. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, the processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) for accelerated matrix and integer operations. The system is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage.

Benchmark results highlight strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reaching top-tier status at 35.73 GB/sec and 14.74 GB/sec respectively. Multi-threaded decompression is also strong at 204.86 GB/sec. For artificial intelligence workloads, the hardware-assisted vector and matrix instructions deliver strong LLM inference speeds, processing 2835.04 tokens/sec for prompt processing on small 135M models and 362.36 tokens/sec for text generation. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 54.55 GB/sec. Standard computational tasks, such as Redis SET operations and PassMark CPU tests, perform in the average tier.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D32ls_v7 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. By omitting local storage and GPUs, resource allocation is optimized strictly for raw CPU throughput. This makes the server highly suitable for compute-intensive tasks, web serving, single-threaded data compression, and lightweight LLM inference or prompt processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and lacks local storage or GPUs, relying on remote Premium Storage. The architecture supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enabling top-tier single-threaded compression performance and strong LLM inference processing speeds. Benchmarks show strong static web serving throughput alongside average multi-core and database performance. This server is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound applications that do not demand high memory density, making it a strong fit for web serving, data compression, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and advanced AMX instructions, delivering top-tier compression and strong web serving performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v7 is a 32-vCPU compute-optimized VM with 64 GB RAM, Intel AMX support, and strong web serving and compression performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at 4.2 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector math
Top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Optimized for compute-bound tasks, web serving, and lightweight LLM inference
